invariants.name: Registry-First Enforcement
invariants.description: All target tables referenced by function must be registered before function creation.
invariants.evidence: lcs.signal_queue and lcs.signal_registry are pre-existing registered tables. lcs.bridge_signal_output is a function, not a table — no registry entry required. outreach.signal_output is a source table owned by outreach hub (not LCS registry scope).

invariants.name: Schema Isolation
invariants.description: Function resides in correct schema and does not create cross-schema DDL.
invariants.evidence: Function created in lcs schema. Reads from outreach and cl schemas (cross-schema SELECT only). Writes only to lcs.signal_queue. No DDL in other schemas.

invariants.name: Existing Function Immutability
invariants.description: Existing lcs.bridge_pressure_signals() must not be modified.
invariants.evidence: WP constraint: 'Replace lcs.bridge_pressure_signals() — do not modify it.' Migration 009 creates a NEW function (bridge_signal_output). bridge_pressure_signals is untouched. Verified: both functions coexist in lcs schema.

invariants.name: Append-Only Write Pattern
invariants.description: Function must INSERT into signal_queue (not UPDATE/DELETE). signal_queue is a staging table with INSERT semantics.
invariants.evidence: Function uses INSERT...ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. No UPDATE or DELETE statements in function body. Consistent with lcs.bridge_pressure_signals pattern.

invariants.name: Rollback Reversibility
invariants.description: Migration must be fully reversible via rollback script.
invariants.evidence: Rollback script: DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS lcs.bridge_signal_output(). Function creation is fully reversible. No data mutations in migration (function definition only).
